verdict_reasoning: HSPA5 (GRP78/BiP) is canonically an ER-luminal chaperone, but it is well-documented to translocate to the cell surface under stress and especially in tumor cells (cancer cell-surface GRP78). Cell-surface GRP78 is an active oncology target with multiple antibody, peptide, and ADC programs engaging it on the outer leaflet of tumor cells. This qualifies as cell_state_induced contextual surface display (ER-stress / oncogenic transformation ecto-pool). Not yes because baseline localization is ER-luminal with its KDEL retention; the surface pool is restricted to stressed/transformed cells.
